# amux

> Open-source Claude Code agent multiplexer — run dozens of parallel AI coding agents unattended via tmux, with a web dashboard, kanban board, self-healing watchdog, and agent-to-agent REST API orchestration.

amux (Agent Multiplexer) is a Python + tmux tool that runs multiple Claude Code sessions in parallel. It provides a mobile-friendly PWA dashboard, atomic kanban board (SQLite), session peek/send, iCal sync, and a REST API for agent orchestration. MIT licensed. No cloud dependency.

## Quick Start

```bash
git clone https://github.com/mixpeek/amux && cd amux
python3 amux-server.py  # opens dashboard at https://localhost:8822
```

## Documentation

- [FAQ](https://amux.io/faq/): What is amux, how does it work, pricing, requirements
- [Getting Started](https://amux.io/guides/getting-started-with-amux/): Installation, first session, dashboard tour
- [Parallel Agents Guide](https://amux.io/guides/running-parallel-claude-agents/): Run multiple agents simultaneously
- [Agent Orchestration](https://amux.io/guides/agent-orchestration-patterns/): Multi-agent coordination patterns
- [Self-Healing Agents](https://amux.io/guides/self-healing-agent-watchdog/): Watchdog auto-restart setup
- [Getting Started with amux](https://amux.io/guides/getting-started/): Install amux and run your first parallel Claude Code agents in under 5 minutes.
- [Running 10+ Agents in Parallel](https://amux.io/guides/running-10-plus-agents/): How to effectively run 10 or more Claude Code agents simultaneously with amux.
- [Self-Healing Configuration](https://amux.io/guides/self-healing-configuration/): Configure amux's self-healing watchdog for auto-compaction, restart, and prompt recovery.
- [Agent-to-Agent Orchestration](https://amux.io/guides/agent-to-agent-orchestration/): How to make Claude Code agents discover peers, delegate tasks, and coordinate work via REST API.
- [Mobile Management via PWA](https://amux.io/guides/mobile-management-pwa/): Install amux as a PWA on your phone to manage AI agents from anywhere.
- [Token Spend Tracking](https://amux.io/guides/token-spend-tracking/): Monitor per-session AI token spend and daily costs with amux's built-in dashboards.
- [Kanban Board for AI Agents](https://amux.io/guides/kanban-board-for-agents/): Use amux's SQLite-backed kanban board to coordinate work across parallel AI agents.
- [Conversation Forking](https://amux.io/guides/conversation-forking/): Clone a Claude Code session's context into multiple new agents to explore different approaches.
- [Git Conflict Avoidance](https://amux.io/guides/git-conflict-avoidance/): How amux detects and prevents git conflicts when multiple AI agents work on the same repo.
- [REST API Reference](https://amux.io/guides/rest-api-reference/): Complete reference for amux's REST API — sessions, board, memory, sync, and orchestration.

- [amux vs LangGraph](https://amux.io/compare/amux-vs-langraph/)

## Key Features

- Parallel Claude Code sessions via tmux (unlimited agents)
- Self-healing watchdog (auto-restarts stalled sessions)
- Web dashboard (PWA, mobile-friendly, dark/light mode)
- Kanban board with atomic task claiming (SQLite)
- Agent-to-agent REST API (POST /api/sessions/NAME/send)
- iCal feed for board items with due dates
- Single-file architecture (amux-server.py — no Docker, no config)
- MIT License — free, open-source, self-hosted
